item.title: Change the case of filenames with Zsh
item.description: Today, after importing photos from my iPhone to my Mac, I noticed their filenames were all uppercase whereas I want them to be all lowercase. This is a task zsh can perform easily with a oneliner.

item.title: My Bookmarking Workflow
item.description: Here is how I set aside links to read them when I have time and classify them with tags to find them easily later on.

item.title: Batch File Renaming With Zsh
item.description: I recently discovered zmv, a builtin feature of zsh that makes batch file renaming, a breeze.

item.title: Octopress 3.0
item.description: I recently upgraded Octopress, the engine running my blog, to version 3.0 with the octopress-genesis-theme. Eagerly waiting for the official release!

item.title: Baked With Octopress
item.description: I finally decided to make the move to Octopress.

item.title: About me
item.description: I’m Eric Bouchut. I write software and I’m really fond of dance, Blockchain and Ruby on Rails. I also have a solid hands-on experience in Java and a real knack for the art of teaching and sharing what I learned. I’m a software developer with over 15 years of experience: 3+ years as a Ruby on Rails and Java developer for LD Mobile editor of netADge, a mobile DSP in the Real Time Bid arena (programmatic advertising).

item.title: Contact Eric Bouchut
item.description: You can get in touch with me via email, consult my professional profile on LinkedIn and keep up with my news on Twitter, Github.
